assets.name: lecture_05_figure_02.png
assets.rationale: This frame gives the clearest and most complete view of the board content in the subtitle window. The periodicity equation is legible, the horizontal segment marked by L is visible as a supporting schematic, and Susskind is not yet significantly blocking either element. The later frames introduce more occlusion from his arm or body, while the earlier frame is slightly less settled compositionally.
assets.subtitle_excerpt: the wave functions for a momentum eigenstate

assets.name: lecture_05_figure_03.png
assets.rationale: This frame cleanly captures the finished board equation with no lecturer occlusion and preserves both notational forms side by side. The expression is already complete, centered, and easy to read, making it more useful for note writing than an earlier or less settled moment in the same window.
assets.subtitle_excerpt: the integral of psi star psi

assets.name: lecture_05_figure_04.png
assets.rationale: This frame cleanly shows the completed operator equation with the summation index visible and no lecturer occlusion. It is a straightforward algebraic blackboard moment, and this candidate gives a stable, centered view that is well suited for note writing.
assets.subtitle_excerpt: would be to take momentum eigenvectors.

assets.name: lecture_05_figure_06.png
assets.rationale: This frame gives the clearest overall view of the stacked operator relations while keeping the key \(\hat{k}\psi(x)\) line readable. The lecturer’s hand indicates the relevant equation without blocking most of it, and more of the surrounding x-space and k-space correspondence remains visible than in the earlier candidates.
assets.subtitle_excerpt: equals k-hat times psi of x.
assets.caption_hint: operator actions in x- and k-space
